coreIssue: This growth conversation is likely about whether Ahmed can define and step into Head of Product leadership through strategic ownership rather than tactical execution.
openingQuestion: What would next-level Head of Product impact look like in your work over the next cycle, beyond keeping delivery moving?
listenFor: whether he names one concrete area of end-to-end ownership versus listing broad leadership traits
whether he pivots quickly into roadmap or delivery mechanics when you ask about influence, trust, or executive communication
if they identify a specific stakeholder or forum where confidence, alignment, or decision framing feels harder than it should
avoid: do not turn the conversation into a review of current delivery performance or project status
do not offer a generic list of leadership habits without tying them to Head of Product scope and decision authority
goodOutcome: You and Ahmed have agreed one Head of Product-level scope he will own more strategically, with a shared definition of what visible leadership looks like in that area.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example where Ahmed defaulted to tactical detail in a senior discussion so you can test whether the pattern is pressure-driven or a scope-definition gap.
